citation_text: California Health & Safety Code §113758 & §114365 — Cottage Food Operations (CFOs) may produce only 'non-potentially hazardous foods' as defined in §113871. Hot prepared meals (e.g. daily tiffin / hot lunch delivery) contain time/temperature-controlled-for-safety (TCS) ingredients and DO NOT qualify for CFO. They require a full Retail Food Facility Health Permit (Class B inspection), a commercial-kitchen build-out, an annual permit fee (~$300–$700), and a 45-day initial inspection cycle.
class_a_allowed_examples: Pre-packaged weekend meal-pack PREORDERS where food is not hot at hand-off
